channel.title: NPR Topics: Business
channel.description: Find the latest business news with reports on Wall Street, interest rates, banking, companies, and U.S. and world financial markets. Subscribe to the Business Story of the Day podcast.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: World Cup fans are missing games after their resale tickets fall through
item.description: The ordeal has left fans forced to either miss out on a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity or scramble to find new tickets, often costing more and for worse seats.
item.content:encoded:
The ordeal has left fans forced to either miss out on a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity or scramble to find new tickets, often costing more and for worse seats.

(Image credit: Shafkat Anowar)
item.dc:creator: Juliana Kim

item.title: Meta plans to release AI-powered prediction market app, documents show
item.description: The company is building an app separate from Facebook and Instagram where people can wager on the outcome of real-world events, using "play money."
item.content:encoded:
The company is building an app separate from Facebook and Instagram where people can wager on the outcome of real-world events, using "play money."

(Image credit: Kelly Sullivan)
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn

item.title: Congress passes the largest housing affordability bill in decades — and Trump cancels the signing
item.description: President Trump canceled a signing ceremony for the bill Wednesday, saying Congress must first pass a strict voter ID bill.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump canceled a signing ceremony for the bill Wednesday, saying Congress must first pass a strict voter ID bill.

(Image credit: Tony Gutierrez)
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Is AI 'one big bubble'? Behind the tech sell-off
item.description: Investors are selling off AI-related stocks as doubts are starting to surface over whether the massive spending on AI is worth the investment and whether it's "one big bubble."
item.content:encoded:
Investors are selling off AI-related stocks as doubts are starting to surface over whether the massive spending on AI is worth the investment and whether it's "one big bubble."

(Image credit: Chris Hondros/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: John Ruwitch

item.title: When falling housing prices are good news — and when they're not
item.description: Denver renters are celebrating falling housing costs. But sometimes cheaper housing is a sign of economic decline. How can you tell the difference?
item.content:encoded:
Denver renters are celebrating falling housing costs. But sometimes cheaper housing is a sign of economic decline. How can you tell the difference?

(Image credit: Scott Olson)
item.dc:creator: Greg Rosalsky

item.title: Did you know? Alan Greenspan and Ayn Rand were close friends
item.description: One of the most intellectually important relationships in the life of the late Fed chair Alan Greenspan was with his close friend, the formidable novelist and libertarian thinker Ayn Rand.
item.content:encoded:
One of the most intellectually important relationships in the life of the late Fed chair Alan Greenspan was with his close friend, the formidable novelist and libertarian thinker Ayn Rand.

(Image credit: David Hume Kennerly)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas

item.title: Alan Greenspan, the legendary former Federal Reserve chair, dies
item.description: During his chairmanship, Greenspan was celebrated as possibly the best central banker in history. But later, his reputation was tarnished by the worst financial crisis since the Great Depression.
item.content:encoded:
During his chairmanship, Greenspan was celebrated as possibly the best central banker in history. But later, his reputation was tarnished by the worst financial crisis since the Great Depression.

(Image credit: Paul J. Richards)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Here is how SpaceX could turn up in your current investments
item.description: SpaceX stock hit the market last week. Here's how it could turn up in your retirement or investment portfolio.
item.content:encoded: SpaceX stock hit the market last week. Here's how it could turn up in your retirement or investment portfolio.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Poll: Most Americans have the summer blues about Trump and the economy
item.description: A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds a record low share of Americans approve of President Trump's job performance and his handling of the economy heading into the summer before a key midterm election.
item.content:encoded:
A new NPR/PBS News/Marist poll finds a record low share of Americans approve of President Trump's job performance and his handling of the economy heading into the summer before a key midterm election.

(Image credit: Charlie Neibergall)
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler

item.title: Here's how much the Iran war cost -- and how its effects will linger
item.description: More than three months after the U.S. and Israel launched their war on Iran, the costs and aftereffects are felt around the world.
item.content:encoded:
More than three months after the U.S. and Israel launched their war on Iran, the costs and aftereffects are felt around the world.

(Image credit: Firdous Nazir)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley
